Ward infrastructure includes sterilization room, septic rooms, recovery/awakening rooms, examination and dressing rooms, and a reception area. Capabilities: Le Mete NGO Ghana owns a 4-hectare site donated by village chiefs of Nanton, registered as the property of Le Mete NGO Ghana. The center is integrated with the Tamale Teaching Hospital and operates under a co-ownership/management agreement with the University and the hospital. Led by Dr. Akis Afoko, who is president of Le Mete NGO Ghana. Population served by the center is estimated at 7 million. The project is supported by the Ghana Health Service and the Ministries of Education and Health. Estimated total project cost is 500,000 euros; Italy-based donations total 159,731 euros; Ghana-based donations total 120,000 euros; materials/equipment donations total around 110,000 euros. Phase status: Phase 1 and Phase 2 completed with two operating theatres; Phase 3 maternal-infant department with 18 beds; Phase 4 expansion completed; Phase 5 in progress to build additional wards, mortuary, auditorium and classrooms. capabilities: The Thyroid Ghana Foundation is a non-governmental organization focused on creating awareness of thyroid disorders in Ghana and providing treatment.
Its mission includes creating awareness of thyroid diseases in Ghana.
Its mission includes creating opportunities for early detection of thyroid problems.
Its mission includes supporting thyroid research and institutions involved in thyroid disease management.
Its mission includes providing access to affordable treatment and advocating for improved healthcare practices for thyroid disease patients in Ghana.
The foundation intends to develop a registry for all thyroid patients in the country starting from the Greater Accra region.
The foundation intends to build a thyroid care unit within Korle-Bu Teaching Hospital.
The foundation plans to develop supportive programmes and facilities for people affected by thyroid diseases.
Location: 112, Lane 10, Onyinase Street Awoshie, Accra.

Board Chair: Rev. Prof. Patrick F. Ayeh-Kumi.
Head of Operations: Mr. Leslie Chartey Kumahlor.
Public Relations Officer: Dr. Joyce Emefa Addo-Klah.
Consultant Dietician: Frank Anyimadu.
Research Coordinator: Justice Kwesi Baah.
Staff page dated 05.24.2018.
Offers awareness events, partnership opportunities, and volunteering opportunities.
